title: Ideas for Practicing Participating Participate with awareness of connection to the universe:

pages.text:
59
From DBT Skills Training Handouts and Worksheets, Second Edition by Marsha M. Linehan. Copyright 2015 by Marsha M. Linehan. Permission
to photocopy this handout is granted to purchasers of this book for personal use only (see copyright page for details).
Mindfulness Handout 4c   (Mindfulness Worksheets 2вҖ“2c, 4вҖ“4b)
Ideas for Practicing Participating
Participate with awareness of connection to the universe:
  1.	пҒұ	Focus your attention on where your body touches an object (floor or ground, air molecules,
a chair or armrest, your bed sheets and covers, your clothes, etc.). Try to see all the ways
you are connected to and accepted by that object. Consider the function of that object with
relation to you. That is, consider what the object does for you. Consider its kindness in
doing that. Experience the sensation of touching the object, and focus your entire attention
on that kindness until a sense of being connected or loved or cared for arises in your heart.
Examples: Focus your attention on your feet touching the ground. Consider the kindness of
the ground holding you up, providing a path for you to get to other things, not letting you fall
away from everything else. Focus your attention on your body touching the chair you sit in.
Consider how the chair accepts you totally, holds you up, supports your back, and keeps
you from falling down on the floor. Focus your attention on the sheets and covers on your
bed. Consider the touch of the sheets and covers holding you, surrounding and keeping
you warm and comfortable. Consider the walls in the room. They keep out the wind and the
cold and the rain. Think of how the walls are connected to you via the floor and the air in the
room. Experience your connection to the walls that provide you with a secure place to do
things. Go hug a tree. Think of how you and the tree are connected. Life is in you and in the
tree and both of you are warmed by the sun, held by the air and supported by the earth. Try
and experience the tree loving you by providing something to lean on, or by shading you.
  2.	пҒұ	Dance to music.
  3.	пҒұ	Sing along with music you are listening to.
  4.	пҒұ	Sing in the shower.
  5.	пҒұ	Sing and dance while watching TV.
  6.	пҒұ	Jump out of bed and dance, or sing before getting dressed.
  7.	 пҒұ	Go to a church that sings, and join in the singing.
  8.	пҒұ	Play karaoke with friends or at a karaoke club or bar.
  9.	пҒұ	Throw yourself into what another person is saying.
10.	пҒұ	Go running, focusing only on running.
11.	 пҒұ	Play a sport and throw yourself into playing.
12.	пҒұ	Become the count of the breath, becoming only вҖңoneвҖқ when you count 1, becoming only
вҖңtwoвҖқ when you count 2, and so on.
13.	пҒұ	Become a word as you slowly say the word over and over and over.
14.	пҒұ	Take a class in improvisational acting.
15.	пҒұ	Take a dance class.
16. Other:

Mindfulness Handout 4c   (Mindfulness Worksheets 2вҖ“2c, 4вҖ“4b)
Ideas for Practicing Participating
Participate with awareness of connection to the universe:
  1.	пҒұ	Focus your attention on where your body touches an object (floor or ground, air molecules,
a chair or armrest, your bed sheets and covers, your clothes, etc.). Try to see all the ways

Examples: Focus your attention on your feet touching the ground. Consider the kindness of

  2.	пҒұ	Dance to music.
  3.	пҒұ	Sing along with music you are listening to.
  4.	пҒұ	Sing in the shower.
  5.	пҒұ	Sing and dance while watching TV.
  6.	пҒұ	Jump out of bed and dance, or sing before getting dressed.

10.	пҒұ	Go running, focusing only on running.

14.	пҒұ	Take a class in improvisational acting.
15.	пҒұ	Take a dance class.
16. Other:

ourspace_shared_life_filter.title: Ideas for Practicing Participating Participate with awareness of connection to the universe:
ourspace_shared_life_filter.shared_facing_summary: Mindfulness is allowed to be tiny, sensory-safe, and done from bed or wheelchair. Noticing without judging counts even when the day is messy.
tiny_steps: Find one neutral thing in the room.
Return to the next safe action.

flare_day_versions.minimum: One breath, one label, one safe message, or one checkbox.
flare_day_versions.medium: Two to five minutes with everything reachable from bed/chair.
flare_day_versions.full: Use the original worksheet/handout when both bodies and brains have capacity.

accessibility_modifications: Use eyes-open grounding if closing eyes feels unsafe.
Use quiet, low-scent, low-touch options for MCAS, trauma, and sensory overload.
No posture requirement; lying down or supported sitting is valid.
relationship_and_caregiver_notes: William's needs and Jasper's capacity both count.
Ask before helping; ask before escalating; name the smallest next step.
Pause means pause, not abandonment. I am coming back. I care about us. I need a smaller next step.
What is needed, what capacity exists, what can be smaller, and what can wait?
identity_and_trauma_filter: Use William's masculine language and Jasper's they/them language.
Use consent-based, non-shaming, body-neutral language unless a person has explicitly chosen a term.
Do not turn symptoms, dysphoria, trauma responses, clutter, fatigue, or emotional intensity into moral failure.
avoid_or_adjust: Do not force stillness when it increases pain, panic, dysphoria, or trauma activation.

ourspace_shared_revision_metadata.copyright_note: Extracted from user-provided PDF: DBT Skills Training Handouts and Worksheets, Second Edition by Marsha M. Linehan. Respect source copyright and purchaser-use limits.
